# killpy

> Canonical project docs for `killpy`, a Python environment cleaner and disk usage CLI/TUI.

## Project summary

`killpy` helps developers find, inspect, and remove unused Python environments and related disk usage.

It supports detection for:

- virtualenv and `.venv` directories
- directories containing `pyvenv.cfg`
- Conda environments
- Poetry environments
- `pipx` package environments
- `pyenv` versions
- Pipenv, Hatch, tox, and uv environments
- cache directories and Python build artifacts in scanner and CLI flows

## Product facts

- Primary audience: Python developers and CLI users trying to free disk space
- Interfaces: Textual TUI plus scriptable CLI commands
- Key commands: `killpy`, `killpy list`, `killpy delete`, `killpy stats`, `killpy clean`
- Safety model: scanning is non-destructive; external tools are used for Conda and `pipx` deletion when possible
- TUI scope: environment results plus a dedicated `pipx` tab
- `killpy clean` scope: recursive `__pycache__` removal under a target path

- Home: /killpy/

- GitHub: https://github.com/Tlaloc-Es/killpy
- Docs: https://tlaloc-es.github.io/killpy/
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/killpy/

## Search intents

- delete old Python virtual environments
- find unused Conda environments
- inspect Poetry environment size
- clean `pipx` package environments
- remove Python caches and build artifacts
- free disk space from Python tooling
